title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. I–IX
summary: CME00004 is a selective U-M electronic compilation of six articles published as nine numbered installments in Englische Studien 7–11 (1884–1888). The articles edit several discontiguous portions of the Auchinleck manuscript and its detached Edinburgh fragments; installment II also prints Cambridge Ff.2.38 with Harley 5396 variants, and installment IX prints Auchinleck and Vernon in parallel with Simeon variants. Complete public journal volumes and the complete exposed Auchinleck and Vernon facsimile sets are cached locally, with exact work portions recorded inside the larger objects.
review_notes: The U-M discovery title says 1884–1889, but direct title-page inspection dates Englische Studien 11 to 1888; the source-article span is therefore 1884–1888.
A complete facsimile means the provider's complete exposed object or image inventory, not a claim to preservation-master resolution or a redistribution license.
The NLS project facsimile covers the surviving codex surfaces and detached fragments represented by its 701-image public inventory; CME00004 occupies several discontiguous ranges within it.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00004
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00004.
The submodule source is distinct from the manifest files in this repository.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00004
entities.description: A public electronic text selecting the Middle English editions and much local apparatus from six Englische Studien articles. It omits substantial introductions and discussions and serializes the two parallel King of Tars columns as consecutive version divisions.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. I–II (1884)

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. I. Lob der Frauen. II. A Peniworth of Witte,' Englische Studien 7 (1884): 101–125.
bibliographic.title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. I. Lob der Frauen. II. A Peniworth of Witte

notes: Installment I occupies pp. 101–110; installment II occupies pp. 111–125, with the edited texts beginning on pp. 103 and 113 respectively.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. III (1885)

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. III. Zwei Fragmente von King Richard,' Englische Studien 8 (1885): 115–119.
bibliographic.title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. III. Zwei Fragmente von King Richard

entities.label: Max Schwarz, Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. IV (1885)

bibliographic.citation: Max Schwarz, 'Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. IV. Die Assumptio Mariae in der Schweifreimstrophe,' Englische Studien 8 (1885): 427–464.
bibliographic.title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. IV. Die Assumptio Mariae in der Schweifreimstrophe

notes: CME00004 encodes the edited Auchinleck poem on pp. 448–457 rather than Schwarz's complete study.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. V–VII (1886)

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. V–VII,' Englische Studien 9 (1886): 42–50.
bibliographic.title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. V–VII

notes: V: On the Seven Deadly Sins, pp. 42–46; VI: Paternoster, pp. 47–49; VII: Psalm L, pp. 49–50.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. VIII (1886)

bibliographic.title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. VIII. Die Feinde des Menschen

entities.label: F. Krause, Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. IX: The King of Tars (1888)

bibliographic.citation: F. Krause, 'Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. IX. The King of Tars,' Englische Studien 11 (1888): 1–62.
bibliographic.title: Kleine Publicationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. IX. The King of Tars

notes: Manuscripts and linguistic/source discussion occupy pp. 1–32; the Auchinleck and Vernon texts appear in parallel on pp. 33–62 with Simeon variants below the Vernon column.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Englische Studien 7 (1884)

Internet Archive scan of Englische Studien 9 (1886)

National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)
entities.description: The principal codex from which installments I–II and IV–IX draw discontiguous texts. Installment III instead uses leaves detached from this codex and now held at Edinburgh University Library.

holding.notes: The current codex contains i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios plus modern endleaves; ten detached folios survive in other repositories.

physical_description.extent: i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios, 14 stubs, modern endleaves, and 10 detached folios in other repositories
notes: Probably produced in London; the current codex is incomplete and many leaves or miniatures are lost.

entities.label: University of Edinburgh Library MS 218, Auchinleck fragments
entities.description: Four detached Auchinleck leaves; the second bifolium, ff. 3–4, carries the two King Richard fragments transcribed by Kölbing in installment III.

holding.notes: Two leaves carry Life of Adam and Eve and two carry Richard Coer de Lyon.

notes: Double columns, 44 lines per page; formerly used as binding waste.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century paper household manuscript. Kölbing based the later version of A Peniworth of Witt on its How a Merchant Betrayed His Wife at ff. 57v–59r.

identifiers.scheme: DIMEV/IMEV for How a merchant betrayed his wife

holding.notes: No repository-owned exact item record was located in the public search; the exact-content handlist is scholarly rather than substituted as an institutional catalog.

physical_description.extent: iii + 247 + i leaves in its present state
notes: Two scribes; current foliation accounts for lost material and later rebinding.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 5396
entities.description: A composite miscellany whose mid-fifteenth-century Middle English verse unit is ff. 271–311v. Kölbing collated the first 167 lines of its A Penyworth of Wytt text from f. 286 onward against Cambridge Ff.2.38.

date_statements.label: Middle English verse unit
date_statements.value: mid fifteenth century; date 1456 occurs on f. 306

holding.notes: The exact poem is identified by Kölbing at f. 286 onward; the current catalog describes the enclosing verse collection rather than itemizing this poem.

physical_description.extent: 311 folios; Middle English verse unit ff. 271–311v
notes: Composite of four codicological units.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: The Vernon version of The King of Tars at ff. 304vb–307ra, printed by Krause in parallel with Auchinleck.

holding.notes: Digital Bodleian supplies a complete 710-canvas object including covers and spine.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i folios, incomplete; originally viii + 413
notes: Produced in England, probably the West Midlands; Middle English religious and didactic compilation with over 350 items.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript)
entities.description: The third King of Tars witness, ff. 126rc–128va. Krause treated it as a close copy of Vernon and recorded its minor variants beneath the Vernon column.

holding.notes: Current exact component description identifies The King of Tars at ff. 126r(c)–128v(a).

notes: Middle English, Middle French, and Latin miscellany associated with the Vernon manuscript; probably produced in the West Midlands.

entities.label: Auchinleck Manuscript project digital facsimile
entities.description: The NLS-supported project exposes a full transcription and public facsimile links for 701 surviving codex and detached-fragment surfaces through IIIF Image API services. No Presentation manifest is offered, so the complete verified URL inventory is bundled locally.

physical_description.extent: 701 public leaf, stub, and detached-fragment images in the verified project inventory
notes: Representative inspection includes ordinary pages, rubricated leaves, and the painted King of Tars opening; color and gold/red/blue decoration are visibly preserved.

entities.reproduction_format: Individual IIIF JPEG images and Image Information documents

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 facsimile (Scolar Press, 1979)
entities.description: A printed whole-manuscript facsimile introduced by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson. No complete public digital copy was verified.

bibliographic.citation: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, with an introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson (London: Scolar Press, 1979).
bibliographic.title: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38

notes: Color/fidelity characteristics were not verified from a copy.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: A 710-canvas digital object spanning the upper cover through spine/lower cover. The King of Tars occupies six canvases, fol. 304v through fol. 307r.

notes: Representative inspection of fols. 304v and 307r shows the three-column text, colored initials, rubrication, and ornamental borders in color.

entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

local_copies.label: Complete public IIIF image-inventory bundle for the Auchinleck manuscript and detached fragments
notes: The verified 701-image list was enumerated from all manuscript-item facsimile links in the provider's complete Auchinleck project; the provider exposes Image API services but no Presentation manifest.
ZIP contains the normalized exact source URL list, inventory.json, and every image in that list at the provider resource profile.
Coverage is complete for the public project inventory and includes detached fragments; it does not assert that covers, spine, edges, or preservation-master files are exposed.
Caching records a research copy only and does not override the project's publication and commercial-use restrictions.

work_portion.label: CME00004's discontiguous Auchinleck texts and Edinburgh MS 218 King Richard fragments
locators: IX The King of Tars: ff. 7ra–13vb; inventory items 13–26
V On the Seven Deadly Sins, VI Paternoster, and IV Assumption: ff. 70ra–78ra around damaged f. 72A; inventory items 143–161
II A Peniworþ of Witt: f. 256A stub–259rb; project inventory items 523–530 include the supplemental 256 surfaces through f. 259r
VII Dauid þe King: ff. 280rb–280vb; inventory items 574–575
VIII The Four Foes of Mankind: f. 303rb–303vb; inventory items 622–623
I Alphabetical Praise of Women: ff. 324ra–325vb; inventory items 664–667
III King Richard: Edinburgh University Library MS 218 fragment 2, ff. 3ra–4vb; inventory items 690–693
notes: The start/end links bound the earliest and latest main-codex ranges only; they do not imply that all intervening leaves belong to CME00004.
The King Richard source is the detached Edinburgh fragment, not the main-codex ff. 326–327 or St Andrews fragments also present in the full project.
The official catalog's uncertainty around the damaged f. 72A boundary is retained rather than converted into a false precise folio assignment.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Six complete source articles totaling 143 printed pages
editorial_practices.representation: Selected Middle English texts, notes, headings, and limited afterword material
editorial_practices.description: The XML revision history explicitly records removal of incomplete introductions, an incomplete appendix, incomplete notes, and a lengthy introduction; source comparison confirms that large article discussions are absent.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Krause's page-by-page parallel Auchinleck and Vernon columns on pp. 33–62
editorial_practices.representation: Two consecutive DIV2 version divisions with repeated PB N 33–62 milestones
editorial_practices.description: The encoding makes witness versions consecutive rather than parallel; these divisions are representations of witnesses, not intrinsic chapters of The King of Tars.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Cambridge Ff.2.38 and fragmentary Harley 5396 versions
editorial_practices.representation: Cambridge-based text with mostly minor Harley variants in notes
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing states that Cambridge is the more complete and carefully written witness and prints it as the base, while recording Harley's first 167 lines as H variants.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Auchinleck (A), Vernon (V), and Simeon (C) King of Tars witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: A and V in parallel columns with C variants beneath V
editorial_practices.description: Krause says A and V descend from a lost common exemplar and therefore prints them in exact parallel rather than constructing an eclectic critical text; C is collated as the close Vernon-related copy.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript abbreviations in A and V
editorial_practices.description: Krause explicitly states that manuscript spelling is retained and abbreviations are resolved in italic type; the XML preserves many of these spans as HI REND='italic'.
notes: This direct statement applies to installment IX and should not automatically be generalized to every earlier installment's italic typography.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME source XML for CME00004
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR; IDG; BODY/DIV1 and DIV2; PB, HEAD, LG, L, NOTE, and MILESTONE elements
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00004 and preserves the selected texts, editorial notes, source page and manuscript milestones, and explicit revision notes about omitted incomplete introductions and apparatus.

evidence.citation: Local structural and content inspection of CME00004.xml
evidence.locator: Top-level DIV1, child DIV2, and descendant L/LG/PB/NOTE counts
evidence.summary: Verified the nine-installment/ten-DIV1 mismatch, per-part line-group metrics, omitted source pages, and the consecutive serialization of the parallel King of Tars versions.

open_questions.question: Does the current NLS permanent digital object expose covers, spine, endleaves, and other binding surfaces beyond the 701-image Auchinleck project inventory, and is a direct whole-object download available?
next_steps: Ask NLS whether Adv.MS.19.2.1D has an exportable complete-object file or Presentation manifest.
If additional binding surfaces are exposed, cache the complete physical-object sequence and retain the present project bundle as a separately identified transcription-linked facsimile.
notes: The current bundle is complete for the project's verified 701 public leaf/fragment surfaces and already includes every CME00004 Auchinleck surface.

open_questions.question: Can Cambridge University Library supply or authorize a complete digital facsimile of MS Ff.2.38, rather than only mediated viewing or individual ordered images?
